text.title: Test as Living Documentation Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed when applying Test as Living Documentation. Each ties a symptom to a root cause and a concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Tests pass but business stakeholders can't read them; misalignment grows.
antipattern.root-cause: test names are camelCase or jargon-heavy
antipattern.fix: Rewrite top-10 critical-path tests in Given/When/Then; stakeholder sample review.

antipattern.symptom: AC orphaned; 50% have no tests; reviewer surprise at QA sign-off.
antipattern.fix: Backfill tests for high-priority AC first; coverage report in PR template.

antipattern.symptom: Doc generator broken for 3 months; reports stale.
antipattern.fix: Fix pipeline; subscribe to CI failure notifications.

antipattern.symptom: Test name and AC drift apart over time.
antipattern.root-cause: test name no longer matches ac.title after AC edits
antipattern.fix: Quarterly: sync names + AC; or automate.

antipattern.symptom: Stakeholder review never happens; tests become dev-only artefact again.
antipattern.fix: Schedule review with PM/BA; embed in quarterly product review.
